title: Foundational Certificate in Finnish Pedagogy and Creative Classroom Practice
duration: 6 modules x 90 minutes + 2-week classroom implementation task
audience: Teachers; Teaching assistants; Tutors; Parents supporting learning; New school leaders
aim: Equip educators with the foundational values, methods and classroom tools required to apply Finnish-inspired pedagogy ethically and practically in everyday teaching.

title: Advanced Specialist in Targeted Intervention and Inclusive Support
duration: 8 modules x 90 minutes + 4-week intervention evidence cycle
audience: Experienced teachers; SENCOs; Intervention leads; Pastoral leaders; Retired teacher mentors; Academic coordinators
aim: Train educators to design and lead targeted intervention systems aligned with Finnish-inspired equity, inclusion, wellbeing and early support principles.

title: Strategic Educational Leadership Diploma: Finnish-Inspired School Development and Quality Implementation
duration: 8 modules x 90 minutes + 60-day school implementation roadmap
audience: School owners; Principals; Headteachers; Academic directors; Quality assurance leads; Ministry/district officers; Consultants
aim: Equip leaders with the strategic tools to audit, plan, implement, evaluate and sustain Finnish-inspired pedagogy, learning spaces, teacher development, student agency and quality assurance.

title: SISU Preschool Box: Play-Based Early Years Toolkit for Teachers and Parents
audience: Preschool teachers; Kindergarten teachers; Parents; Nursery leaders; Teaching assistants
aim: Enable teachers and parents to design play-based early learning using a Finnish-inspired preschool box of games, teaching aids, movement tasks and child-observation routines.

title: Finnish ECE Implementation and CCE Accreditation Readiness
audience: ECE leaders; Preschool owners; Kindergarten coordinators; School managers; Lead educators
aim: Prepare educators and institutions to implement Finnish ECE principles and build an evidence portfolio suitable for accreditation-readiness discussions with CCE Finland or similar quality bodies.

title: Becoming a Finnish-Inspired School: CCE School Development and Accreditation Readiness
audience: School owners; Principals; Directors; Quality assurance leads; Ministry/district officers
aim: Guide schools step by step through Finnish-inspired readiness for CCE school development, accreditation-quality evidence and a credible school transformation proposal.

title: AI-Enabled Schools: Finnish-Inspired Pedagogy, Ethics and Classroom Transformation
audience: Teachers; School leaders; IT leads; Curriculum leaders; Assessment coordinators
aim: Equip schools to adopt AI responsibly for teaching, learning, administration and assessment while protecting equity, wellbeing and teacher professional judgment.

title: Finland Education Immersion: School Visit Preparation, Experience and Transfer Programme
duration: Pre-course 6 hours + 1 or 2 week Finland immersion + post-course 4 hours
audience: Teachers; School leaders; Owners; Facilitators; Education consultants
aim: Prepare educators to learn ethically from Finnish schools during an immersion visit and transfer relevant practices to their own contexts without superficial copying.

title: Phenomenon-Based Learning Practitioner Certificate
audience: Teachers; Student teachers; Curriculum leaders; Project-based learning facilitators
aim: Develop teachers’ confidence to design, manage and assess phenomenon-based learning projects that promote holistic development, wellbeing, sustainability and lifelong learning.

title: Understanding the Finnish Education System: Principles, Policy and Classroom Practice
audience: Teachers; Parents; School leaders; Owners; Facilitators; Education consultants
aim: Provide a comprehensive understanding of Finland’s education model and how its principles can be adapted ethically in other contexts.

title: Finnish Teaching Strategies You Can Adopt This Term
audience: Teachers; Teaching assistants; Department heads; Instructional coaches
aim: Turn widely discussed Finnish teaching strategies into concrete, ethical classroom routines that can be tried immediately.

title: Classroom Management, Behaviour and Wellbeing: Finnish-Informed Supportive Practice
audience: Teachers; Teaching assistants; Pastoral leaders; SENCOs; New teachers
aim: Train teachers to manage behaviour through predictable routines, dignity, early support, relationship-building and differentiated task design.

title: Managing Mixed-Ability Classrooms: Differentiation, Early Support and Practical Planning
audience: Teachers; Teaching assistants; SENCOs; Tutors; Department heads
aim: Help teachers prepare, teach and assess mixed-ability classes through flexible grouping, scaffolded tasks, formative checks, early support and challenge extension.
